title: Linaria brachyceras
taxonomy: Linaria brachyceras is classified within the family Plantaginaceae. This classification places it within the order Lamiales. Linaria brachyceras is further grouped within the genus Linaria and is a species of the toadflax genus. The specific epithet 'brachyceras' provides additional information about the plant's characteristics. Within the broader classification, Linaria brachyceras is an angiosperm, a flowering plant.
uses: Linaria brachyceras has been traditionally used in folk medicine, primarily in Eastern Europe, for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
